response.justification: Wei Chen is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The mandatory requirement of 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development on Windows is not met — C++ appears only briefly in a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, with no mention in the core skills list. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience, no fixed income or capital markets domain knowledge, and the candidate's entire career trajectory points toward web, mobile, and DevOps work rather than systems programming in financial environments.
strengths: Broad polyglot background and demonstrated ability to pick up new technologies quickly across web, mobile, and embedded domains
Strong DevOps and CI/CD knowledge (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development) which aligns with optional desirable skills
Public speaking and workshop facilitation experience suggests solid communication abilities
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015) versus the 5–10 years mandatory requirement
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain experience whatsoever
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is primarily MongoDB and generic SQL, with no evidence of enterprise RDBMS used in capital markets systems
